horizon: Season of the Open Book, fourth leaf — the signs go up true, and the town must be there when a traveler takes the road in
goals: The road into town (issue #202, filed by the founder): the live world serves no chunks — a visitor at fableton.world finds an empty field where sixteen storied corners stand. Nothing the town does counts until it can be seen; fix the serving before any new telling

Finish Decree 9: seven or eight doors still wear 'unhurried' — Quill, Salt, Stilts, Tick, Widow Hood, the Reformed Wolf, and the two newcomers who arrived after the count (the Prince, the Princess) — settle the tally honestly and name them all; and note the decree's spirit reaches three more signs named for a trade instead of a tale (Greta's 'a baker's day', Reynard's 'a fox's retirement', Tam's 'a lamplighter's day') — a lamplighter whose whole story is one dry match deserves better than his job title

The lane owes the miller a telling (Decree 4): his arrival earned a Chronicle line (PR #191) and fifty-two rumors later not one carries his name — let the lane be caught arguing whose debt it actually is, at its usual lateness

The well's book gets its spine: #172 stands ruled buildable and PR #175 waits at the founder's gate — when ratified, land the wish-ledger schema with fixtures and round-trip tests, then backfill both pages: the bridge wish, paid in full, and the nameless second wish (#180), owed and unnamed, exactly as Winsome entered it

The chance node (#182, ruled buildable): if the founder ratifies, land it schema-first — seeded child, optional otherwise, randomness explicit from the sim PRNG — then let a few of the most clockwork schedules breathe; feedback #178 closes when it lands
The book stays full: the region-lore gate (PR #185) has waited since the eleventh — land it so no seventeenth corner can ever ship silent; Decree 8 is paid and the gate is what keeps it paid
